name: GASTRIC CARCINOMA
definition: invasion limited to mucosa + submucosa (T1 lesion) regardless of lymph node involvement

demographics.Prevalence: declining with 24,000 cases annually in USA
demographics.Histo: adenocarcinoma (95%); rarely squamous cell carcinoma / adenoacanthoma
demographics.Location: mostly distal third of stomach + cardia; 60% on lesser curvature, 10% on greater

distribution: mostly distal third of stomach + cardia; 60% on lesser curvature, 10% on greater
discriminating_features: firmness, rigidity, reduced capacity of stomach, aperistalsis in involved area
granular / polypoid folds with encircling growth

general: firmness, rigidity, reduced capacity of stomach, aperistalsis in involved area
granular / polypoid folds with encircling growth
CT: mass of uniform density / varying attenuation
wall thickness > 6 mm (with gas distension) and 13 mm (with positive contrast material
diffuse low attenuation in mucinous carcinoma
enhancement exclusively in linitis plastica type
nodules of serosal surface ← dilated surface lymphatics
diameter of esophagus at gastroesophageal junction larger than adjacent aorta (DDx: hiatal
lymphadenopathy below level of renal pedicle (3%)
